a:r.a:t: Maturity assessment of foundational barriers across planning & process
Criteria
Observations
Leading Practice
Grade
Plan Design Cycle
Defined planning cadence structure is in place
In-year plan changes occur but are generally managed within existing governance processes
Lead times can vary based on business needs and change complexity
Establish an annual & quarterly planning calendars with milestones, owners, dependencies, and freeze dates
Separate annual design from limited in-year change windows
Use standard templates for plan design summaries, assumptions, and approvals
Include structured review points for business, finance, operations, and payroll readiness
Require readiness sign-off before plan release
Conduct a post-cycle review to capture lessons learned for the next cycle
Target / Quota Setting Methodology
Standard target-setting frameworks exist across roles and channels
Local variation is applied to reflect market and site-specific conditions
Consistent monthly adjustments serve as major pain point for field and erode confidence in compensation program
Define a standard target-setting methodology for comparable roles and channels
Document core inputs, assumptions, and decision criteria used in target setting
Allow local variation only where supported by approved business rationale
Review outliers through a centralized governance lens before finalization
Keep a version-controlled record of target assumptions and approvals
Revisit target-setting rules periodically to improve consistency and fairness
Explore opportunities to better stabilize targets on monthly basis
Approval Process
No standardized intake process, evaluation criteria, or escalation thresholds
Approvals are chain-based and reactive
Decisions rely too heavily on informal judgment
Introduce one intake template for all plan, target, and policy change requests
Define approval tiers based on materiality, timing, and business impact
Require clear business rationale, expected impact, and effective-date information
Build in checks for Finance, HR, operations, reporting, and payroll implications
Set SLA-based turnaround expectations for review and approval
Maintain an approval log with decision date, approver, and rationale
Change Management & Documentation
Documentation and communication processes exist for plan changes and updates
Opportunity to further standardize documentation and improve cross-functional clarity
Maintain version-controlled plan documents, SOPs, FAQs, and change logs
Prepare a standard release pack for each plan cycle or major update
Communicate what changed, why it changed, and when it becomes effective
Pair plan releases with manager enablement and role-based communications
Archive prior versions, approvals, and supporting decisions for reference
Monitor early questions / disputes post-release to identify documentation gaps
Compensation Governance Assessment
Governance Structure
Planning & Process
Tools & Data
Limited Issues / Minor Opportunities for Improvement
Moderate Issues / Clear Opportunity for Improvement
Significant Issues Discovered / Action Required to Address Gaps
